Oils from sources such as palm, groundnut, cottonseed, sesame and olive are rich in monounsaturated fattyacids as compared to other oils. Linoleic (n-6) and -linolenic (n-3) acids are the simple PUFA, which are present only in plant foods (Table 6). All vegetable oils (except coconut) are good sources of linoleic(n-6) acid. Soyabean, rapeseed and mustard oils are the only vegetable oils, which contribute significant proportion of a-
linolenic (n-3) acid. Legumes/pulses mustard and fenugreek seeds and green leafy vegetables are also good sources of a-linolenic (n-3) acid (Table 7). On the other hand, fish
